docs.title: Welcome to Strike7
docs.text: Strike7 is an autonomous penetration testing platform. Deploy on-prem Striker nodes on your network, launch AI-driven pentests, and get actionable vulnerability findings — all from the command line.

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
# Install the S7 CLI
curl -sL https://get.strike7.ai | bash

# Authenticate
s7 auth login

# Register and install a Striker on your network
s7 striker register my-striker
# Copy the install token from the output, then:
s7 striker install --token <TOKEN>

# Launch a pentest
s7 pentest create --target 10.0.0.0/24 --type network

# Watch it in real-time
s7 pentest watch <strike-id>

# View findings
s7 pentest findings <strike-id>

docs.title: Documentation
docs.text:
• Installation — Install the S7 CLI on macOS or Linux
  • Authentication — Login via browser or API key
  • Configuration — CLI configuration and global flags
  • Striker — Deploy and manage on-prem Striker nodes
  • Pentest — Create, monitor, and review pentests
  • Scope — Manage pentest targets and scope
  • Diagnostics — Troubleshoot connectivity and configuration
  • Update — Keep the CLI up to date

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
┌──────────────┐        ┌────────────────────┐        ┌──────────────┐
│   S7 CLI     │◄──────►│  Strike7 Platform  │◄──────►│   Striker    │
│ (your laptop)│  HTTPS  │   (Cloud SaaS)     │   VPN  │ (your network)│
└──────────────┘        └────────────────────┘        └──────────────┘

The S7 CLI is your control interface. Strikers are Docker containers deployed inside your network that perform the actual pentesting. They connect securely to the Strike7 platform via an encrypted VPN tunnel.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: The S7 CLI stores configuration in ~/.s7/config.yaml. You can manage it with the s7 config commands or by editing the file directly.

docs.title: Show Configuration
docs.text:
s7 config show

Output:

API URL:  https://internal.api.strike7.ai  (default)
Org ID:   xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx  (config)
Config:   /Users/you/.s7/config.yaml

The source of each value is shown in parentheses: default, config (from config file), or flag (from command-line flag).

Add --json for JSON output.

docs.title: Set a Value
docs.text: s7 config set <key> <value>

docs.title: Available Keys
docs.text: Key Description Example api_url API base URL https://internal.api.strike7.ai org_id Organization ID xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Set a custom API URL
s7 config set api_url https://api.custom.example.com

# Set organization ID
s7 config set org_id xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx

Output:

Set api_url = https://api.custom.example.com

docs.title: Reset Configuration
docs.text:
Delete the config file and start fresh:

s7 config reset

You will be prompted for confirmation. Use --force to skip:

s7 config reset --force

Output:

Configuration reset.

docs.title: Config File Format
docs.text:
~/.s7/config.yaml:

api_url: https://internal.api.strike7.ai
org_id: xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx

docs.title: Global Flags
docs.text:
These flags override config values for any command:

Flag Description --org-id Override organization ID --api-url Override API base URL --json Output as JSON -v, --verbose Verbose output

Precedence: command-line flags > config file > defaults

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Use a different org for one command
s7 pentest list --org-id other-org-id

# Point to a different API
s7 striker list --api-url https://staging.api.strike7.ai

# Get JSON output from any command
s7 striker list --json

docs.title: File Locations
docs.text: Path Purpose ~/.s7/config.yaml CLI configuration ~/.s7/credentials.json Authentication credentials ~/.s7/strikers/ Striker installations

docs.title: Diagnostic Commands
docs.text: Run diagnostics to troubleshoot connectivity, firewall, configuration, and system readiness issues.

docs.title: Connection Test
docs.text:
Test connectivity to the Strike7 API (DNS, TCP, TLS, HTTP health):

s7 diag connection

Output:

Testing connectivity to https://internal.api.strike7.ai...

✓ DNS resolution
✓ TCP connection
✓ TLS handshake
✓ HTTP health check

All connection checks passed.

docs.title: Custom API URL
docs.text: s7 diag connection --api-url https://custom-api.example.com

docs.title: Firewall Check
docs.text:
Verify that all required outbound endpoints are reachable:

s7 diag firewall

Output:

Checking outbound access...

✓ Strike7 API (HTTPS)
✓ AWS ECR (Docker pulls)
✓ AWS S3 (CLI updates)

All endpoints reachable.

docs.title: Configuration Check
docs.text:
Validate local configuration and credentials:

s7 diag config

Output:

Checking configuration in /Users/you/.s7...

✓ Config directory exists
✓ config.yaml present and valid
✓ credentials.json present
✓ File permissions secure (0600)

Configuration looks good.

docs.title: Preflight Check
docs.text:
Run the full system preflight — the same checks that s7 striker install runs before installation:

s7 diag preflight

Output:

Running preflight checks...

✓ Docker installed and running
✓ Docker Compose available
✓ Sufficient disk space (15 GB free)
✓ Kernel version supported
✓ Outbound HTTPS connectivity
✓ ECR registry reachable

All preflight checks passed.

Tip

Run s7 diag preflight before installing a Striker to catch issues early.

docs.title: Run All Diagnostics
docs.text:
s7 diag connection
s7 diag firewall
s7 diag config
s7 diag preflight

docs.title: Verbose Output
docs.text:
Add -v for detailed diagnostic information:

s7 diag connection -v

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: The S7 CLI (s7) is your primary interface for interacting with the Strike7 platform. It supports macOS and Linux on both Intel and ARM architectures.

docs.title: One-Line Install
docs.text:
curl -sL https://get.strike7.ai | bash

This automatically detects your platform and architecture, downloads the correct binary, verifies the SHA256 checksum, and installs to /usr/local/bin/s7.

docs.title: Install + Striker Setup
docs.text:
If you already have an install token from s7 striker register, you can install the CLI and set up a Striker in one step:

curl -sL https://get.strike7.ai | bash -s -- --token <TOKEN>

docs.title: Manual Installation
docs.text:
Download the binary for your platform:

Platform Architecture Binary Linux x86-64 (amd64) s7_linux_amd64 Linux ARM64 s7_linux_arm64 macOS Intel (amd64) s7_darwin_amd64 macOS Apple Silicon (arm64) s7_darwin_arm64

Binaries are hosted on S3: https://s7-cli-releases.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/latest/s7_<os>_<arch>

# Example: macOS Apple Silicon
curl -sL https://s7-cli-releases.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/latest/s7_darwin_arm64 -o s7
chmod +x s7
sudo mv s7 /usr/local/bin/s7

docs.title: Checksum Verification
docs.text:
Each binary has a matching .sha256 file:

curl -sL https://s7-cli-releases.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/latest/s7_darwin_arm64.sha256
# Verify:
shasum -a 256 s7

docs.title: Verify Installation
docs.text:
s7 version
s7 --help

docs.title: Command Structure
docs.text: s7 [command] [subcommand] [flags]

docs.title: Global Flags
docs.text: Flag Description --org-id Organization ID (overrides config) --api-url API base URL (overrides config) --json Output as JSON -v, --verbose Verbose output -h, --help Help for any command

docs.title: Available Commands
docs.text: Command Description auth Authentication (login, logout, status) striker Manage on-prem Striker nodes pentest Create and manage pentests scope Manage pentest scope and targets config CLI configuration diag Run diagnostic checks update Update CLI to latest version version Print version and build info

docs.title: Updating
docs.text:
# Check for updates
s7 update --check

# Update to latest version
s7 update

See Update for more details.

docs.title: Uninstalling
docs.text:
sudo rm /usr/local/bin/s7
rm -rf ~/.s7

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
1. Authenticate with your Strike7 account
  2. Register a Striker on your network
  3. Create a pentest and start testing

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text: All S7 CLI commands require authentication. The CLI supports two methods: browser-based OAuth login and API key authentication.

docs.title: Browser Login (Recommended)
docs.text:
Opens your default browser for secure OAuth authentication:

s7 auth login

Output:

Opening browser for authentication...
Waiting for login... (or visit: https://game-redbird-99.clerk.accounts.dev/...)

Logged in successfully (org: xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx)

docs.title: API Key Login (Headless)
docs.text:
For servers, CI/CD pipelines, or machines without a browser:

s7 auth login --api-key sk_abc123...

Output:

Validating API key...
Logged in successfully (org: xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx)

API Key Generation

Generate an API key from the Strike7 dashboard under Settings > API Keys.

docs.title: Logout
docs.text:
Remove stored credentials:

s7 auth logout

Output:

Logged out.

docs.title: Status
docs.text:
Check your current authentication status:

s7 auth status

Output:

Logged in (org: xxxxxxxx-xxxx, type: browser, expires: 2026-04-14 10:30)

docs.title: Whoami
docs.text:
Alias for s7 auth status:

s7 auth whoami

docs.title: Credential Storage
docs.text:
Credentials are stored in two files under ~/.s7/:

File Purpose ~/.s7/credentials.json Auth token and org ID ~/.s7/config.yaml API URL and org settings

Both files are created with 0600 permissions (readable only by the owner).

credentials.json format:

{
  "token": "sk_... or JWT",
  "orgId": "xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx",
  "expiresAt": "2026-04-14T10:30:00Z"
}

docs.title: Using API Keys
docs.text:
API keys are recommended for:

  • CI/CD pipelines
  • Automated scripts
  • Headless servers and Strikers
  • Service accounts
# Set API key and use the CLI
s7 auth login --api-key sk_abc123...
s7 pentest list

Security

Keep your API key secure. It provides full access to your organization's pentests and Strikers.

docs.title: Pentest Commands
docs.text: Create, monitor, and manage penetration tests from the command line.

docs.title: Create a Pentest
docs.text:
Create a new pentest and automatically start execution:

s7 pentest create --target <target> [flags]

docs.title: Flags
docs.text: Flag Description Required --target Target to pentest (IP, CIDR, or URL) Yes --type Pentest type: web or network (default: web) No --striker Striker ID or name to use No

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Web application pentest
s7 pentest create --target https://app.example.com

# Network pentest with a specific Striker
s7 pentest create --target 10.0.0.0/24 --type network --striker datacenter-striker

# JSON output
s7 pentest create --target https://app.example.com --json

Output:

Strike created: stk_abc12345
Status: pending
Run started: run_def67890
Watch: s7 pentest watch stk_abc12345

The CLI automatically executes the pentest after creation. If execution fails, it provides a manual start command.

docs.title: List Pentests
docs.text:
s7 pentest list

Output:

ID        TYPE      STATUS      CREATED
stk_abc1  web       completed   2026-01-25 10:30
stk_def2  network   running     2026-01-26 09:00
stk_ghi3  web       pending     2026-01-26 09:15

Add --json for JSON output.

docs.title: Show Pentest Details
docs.text:
s7 pentest show <id>

Output:

ID:      stk_abc12345-xxxx-xxxx
Type:    web
Status:  completed
Created: 2026-01-25 10:30:00

Runs (2):
  RUN ID    STATUS      STARTED
  run_abc1  completed   2026-01-25 10:30
  run_def2  completed   2026-01-26 14:00

docs.title: Watch in Real-Time
docs.text:
Stream execution events as they happen:

s7 pentest watch <strike-id>

docs.title: Flags
docs.text:
Flag Description --events-url Custom events API URL (default: production)

Output:

Watching run run_abc1 (Ctrl+C to stop)

--- reconnaissance ---
  [tool] nmap_scan
  [tool] subdomain_enum

--- exploitation ---
  [tool] sql_injection_test
  ** FINDING [CRITICAL] SQL Injection in /api/users endpoint
  [tool] xss_probe
  ** FINDING [HIGH] Reflected XSS in search parameter

Run complete: completed

The watch command connects to the events API via Server-Sent Events (SSE) and displays phase changes, tool calls, findings, and completion status.

docs.title: Stop a Pentest
docs.text:
s7 pentest stop <id>

Output:

Strike stopped.

docs.title: View Findings
docs.text:
View vulnerabilities discovered during a pentest:

s7 pentest findings <strike-id> [run-id]

If no run-id is provided, findings from the latest run are shown.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Latest run findings
s7 pentest findings stk_abc12345

# Specific run
s7 pentest findings stk_abc12345 run_def67890

# JSON output
s7 pentest findings stk_abc12345 --json

Output:

SEVERITY    TYPE                  TITLE
CRITICAL    sql_injection         SQL Injection in /api/users endpoint
HIGH        xss                   Reflected XSS in search parameter
MEDIUM      missing_headers       Missing security headers on /api
LOW         info_disclosure       Server version exposed in headers

docs.title: Workflow Example
docs.text:
# 1. Authenticate
s7 auth login

# 2. Create and run a web pentest
s7 pentest create --target https://app.example.com

# 3. Watch execution in real-time
s7 pentest watch <strike-id>

# 4. Review findings
s7 pentest findings <strike-id>

# 5. Export as JSON for integration
s7 pentest findings <strike-id> --json > findings.json

docs.title: Scope Management
docs.text: Manage the targets and scope of a pentest. Scope entries define what the Striker is allowed to test.

docs.title: Add a Scope Entry
docs.text: s7 scope add <strike-id> <type> <value>

docs.title: Supported Types
docs.text: Type Description Example cidr IP range in CIDR notation 10.0.0.0/24 domain Domain name example.com ip Single IP address 192.168.1.100 url Specific URL https://app.example.com

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Add a CIDR range
s7 scope add stk_abc12345 cidr 10.0.0.0/24

# Add a domain
s7 scope add stk_abc12345 domain example.com

# Add a specific URL
s7 scope add stk_abc12345 url https://app.example.com/api

# JSON output
s7 scope add stk_abc12345 ip 192.168.1.100 --json

Output:

Scope added: cidr 10.0.0.0/24 (scp_abc1)

docs.title: List Scope Entries
docs.text:
s7 scope list <strike-id>

Output:

ID        TYPE    VALUE
scp_abc1  cidr    10.0.0.0/24
scp_def2  domain  example.com
scp_ghi3  url     https://app.example.com/api

Add --json for JSON output.

docs.title: Remove a Scope Entry
docs.text:
s7 scope remove <strike-id> <scope-id>

Output:

Scope entry removed.

docs.title: Example Workflow
docs.text:
# Create a pentest
s7 pentest create --target https://example.com

# Add additional targets to scope
s7 scope add <strike-id> domain api.example.com
s7 scope add <strike-id> cidr 10.0.1.0/24

# Verify scope
s7 scope list <strike-id>

# Start the pentest — Striker will test all scope entries
s7 pentest watch <strike-id>

docs.title: Striker Management
docs.text: Strikers are Docker containers deployed on your network to perform penetration testing. They connect securely to the Strike7 platform via an encrypted VPN tunnel (Headscale/Tailscale) and communicate over mTLS-secured gRPC.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The Striker lifecycle:

  1. Register a Striker on the platform — get an install token
  2. Install the Striker locally using the token — sets up Docker, certificates, and VPN
  3. Run pentests — the Striker receives jobs from the platform
  4. Upgrade or uninstall as needed

docs.title: Register a Striker
docs.text:
Register a new Striker with the Strike7 platform:

s7 striker register <name>

Example:

s7 striker register datacenter-striker

Output:

Striker registered: datacenter-striker (stk_abc12345)

Install command:
  s7 striker install --token eyJzd...

Install Token

The install token is a Base64-encoded payload containing the Striker ID, VPN enrollment key, mTLS certificates, and short-lived Docker registry credentials. It is valid for a limited time — install promptly.

docs.title: Install a Striker
docs.text:
Install a Striker on the current machine using the token from register:

s7 striker install --token <TOKEN>

docs.title: Flags
docs.text: Flag Description --token Install token from s7 striker register (required) --force Force reinstall if already installed

docs.title: What Happens
docs.text:
The installer runs an 8-step process:

  1. Preflight checks — Docker installed, disk space, kernel version, network access
  2. Create directory — ~/.s7/strikers/<strikerId>/
  3. Write mTLS certificates — server.crt, server.key, ca.crt
  4. Generate docker-compose.yml — with Headscale Tailscale VPN configuration
  5. Docker ECR login — using short-lived credentials from the install token
  6. Pull Striker image — from AWS ECR
  7. Start container — docker compose up -d
  8. Health check — polls container status (up to 60 seconds)

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Docker must be installed and running
  • Disk space: at least 2 GB free
  • Network: outbound HTTPS access (no inbound rules needed)

docs.title: One-Line Install
docs.text:
Install the S7 CLI and a Striker in one command:

curl -sL https://get.strike7.ai | bash -s -- --token <TOKEN>

docs.title: List Strikers
docs.text:
s7 striker list

Output:

ID        NAME                  STATUS    IP             LAST SEEN
stk_abc1  datacenter-striker    online    100.64.0.5     2m ago
stk_def2  cloud-vpc-striker     offline   -              3d ago

Add --json for JSON output.

docs.title: Striker Status
docs.text:
Check a specific Striker's health:

s7 striker status <id|name>

Output:

Name:      datacenter-striker
ID:        stk_abc12345-xxxx-xxxx
Status:    online
IP:        100.64.0.5
Version:   1.2.0
Last Seen: 2m ago

docs.title: Test Connection
docs.text:
Test the gRPC connection to a Striker:

s7 striker test <id>

Output:

Connection test: ok

docs.title: Upgrade a Striker
docs.text:
Pull the latest Docker image and restart:

s7 striker upgrade <id>

docs.title: Flags
docs.text:
Flag Description --path Path to docker-compose.yml (auto-detected if omitted)

The upgrade fetches fresh ECR credentials from the API, pulls the latest image, and restarts the container.

docs.title: Automatic Updates (Watchtower)
docs.text:
Every Striker installation includes a Watchtower sidecar that automatically pulls and applies new images from ECR. This works regardless of how pentests are triggered — CLI, SaaS dashboard, or API.

How it works:

  1. Watchtower polls ECR every 5 minutes for new Striker images
  2. ecr-login refreshes ECR authentication tokens every 6 hours (using pull-only IAM credentials)
  3. When a new image is detected, Watchtower performs a rolling restart — pulling the new image and recreating the Striker container
  4. Tailscale VPN state is preserved across restarts (persisted volume)

Key details:

  • Zero-downtime updates via rolling restart (~15-30 seconds during container swap)
  • Label-scoped: Watchtower only manages your Striker container, not other Docker workloads
  • ECR credentials are pull-only (cannot push or modify images)
  • No inbound ports required — all polling is outbound HTTPS

Services running per Striker:

Service Purpose s7-striker-<id> Pentest execution engine + VPN client s7-watchtower-<id> Polls ECR for new images, auto-restarts s7-ecr-login-<id> Refreshes ECR auth tokens (if ECR creds provided)

docs.title: CLI Auto-Upgrade
docs.text:
When you run s7 pentest create, the CLI also checks locally-installed Strikers for newer images. If a newer image is found, the Striker is upgraded before the pentest starts.

  • Runs silently in the background before each pentest
  • Only recreates the container if the image actually changed
  • Failures are non-blocking (printed as warnings with -v)
  • Use s7 striker upgrade for manual control when needed

docs.title: Upgrading Existing Strikers
docs.text: If you installed a Striker before auto-update was available, running s7 striker upgrade <id> will automatically add the Watchtower sidecar to your installation.

docs.title: Uninstall a Striker
docs.text:
Stop the container and remove local files:

s7 striker uninstall <id>

This stops the Docker container, removes the compose file, certificates, and the Striker directory at ~/.s7/strikers/<id>/.

docs.title: Delete a Striker
docs.text:
Deregister a Striker from the platform (does not touch local files):

s7 striker delete <id>

Warning

This removes the Striker from the platform. The Striker will no longer receive jobs. To also remove local files, run s7 striker uninstall first.

docs.title: Network Requirements
docs.text:
Strikers only require outbound connectivity:

Destination Port Protocol Purpose Strike7 API 443 HTTPS API communication AWS ECR 443 HTTPS Docker image pulls Headscale VPN 443 HTTPS VPN tunnel coordination

No inbound firewall rules are required. All connections are initiated by the Striker.

docs.title: File Locations
docs.text: Path Purpose ~/.s7/strikers/<id>/ Striker installation directory ~/.s7/strikers/<id>/docker-compose.yml Docker Compose configuration ~/.s7/strikers/<id>/certs/ mTLS certificates

docs.title: Striker shows "offline"
docs.text:
1. Check if the Docker container is running: docker ps | grep striker
  2. Check container logs: docker logs <container-name>
  3. Run diagnostics: s7 diag preflight
  4. Verify network access: s7 diag firewall

docs.title: Install fails at "Pulling image"
docs.text:
• Ensure Docker is running: docker info
  • Check internet connectivity
  • The ECR credentials in the token may have expired (valid for 12 hours). Re-register to get a fresh token.

docs.title: Force reinstall
docs.text: s7 striker install --token <TOKEN> --force

docs.title: Updating the CLI
docs.text: Keep your S7 CLI up to date to get the latest features and bug fixes.

docs.title: Check for Updates
docs.text:
s7 update --check

Output (update available):

Update available: 1.0.0 → 1.1.0
Run 's7 update' to install.

Output (up to date):

Up to date: 1.1.0

docs.title: Update to Latest
docs.text:
s7 update

Output:

Updated: 1.0.0 → 1.1.0

If the CLI is installed in /usr/local/bin and you don't have write permissions:

sudo s7 update

docs.title: Manual Update
docs.text:
If the update command fails or you have a very old version:

# Remove old version
sudo rm -f /usr/local/bin/s7

# Download and install latest
curl -sL https://get.strike7.ai | bash

docs.title: Version Information
docs.text:
s7 version

Output:

s7 version 1.1.0 (commit: abc1234, built: 2026-03-14)

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text: View release notes at the S7 CLI releases page.
